title: सरस्वती पञ्चरत्नम्
devanagariText:
॥ श्री सरस्वती पञ्चरत्नम् ॥

श्वेतारविन्दासनसंस्थितां श्वेतां श्वेतवस्त्रां वरदाभयाम्।
वीणाधरां पुस्तकहस्तयुग्मां सरस्वतीं प्रणमामि विद्यादाम्॥ १॥

मुक्ताविभूषणधरां हिमकुन्दवर्णां वागीश्वरीं कमलजां विमलां विशालाम्।
बुद्धिप्रदां वरदां ज्ञानमयीं प्रसन्नां सरस्वतीं प्रणमामि कवित्वदाम्॥ २॥

यद्वाक्सुधामधुरवर्षिणि भाग्यधात्रि कान्तिस्मिते कमलदामनिवासयुक्ते।
विद्याप्रदे विबुधवन्दितपादपद्मे सरस्वतीं प्रणमामि शारदाम्बे॥ ३॥

हंसाधिरूढरमणीयमनोहरस्थां भक्तार्तिनाशनपरां कृपयासुपूर्णाम्।
नादब्रह्मस्वरूपिणीं सर्वशास्त्रमातां सरस्वतीं प्रणमामि ज्ञानदाम्॥ ४॥

यस्याः प्रसादमवलम्ब्य जडोऽपि वक्ता कालिदासवरतुल्यकविश्च भूयात्।
शास्त्रेषु योग्यमतिरत्नसमप्रभा सा सरस्वतीं प्रणमामि सुबुद्धिदाम्॥ ५॥

सरस्वतीपञ्चरत्नं यः पठेत्प्रातरुत्थितः।
सर्वविद्यां समासाद्य कविराजो भवेन्नरः॥

॥ इति श्री सरस्वती पञ्चरत्नम् सम्पूर्णम् ॥
transliteration:
|| Shri Saraswati Pancharatnam ||

Shvetaaravindaasanasamsthitam Shvetam Shvetavastram Varadaabhayam |
Veenadharam Pustakahastayugmam Sarasvatim Pranamami Vidyadam || 1 ||

[...]

|| Iti Shri Saraswati Pancharatnam Sampurnam ||
hindiMeaning:
श्वेत कमलासन, श्वेतवस्त्र, वीणा-पुस्तकधारिणी विद्यादा सरस्वती को प्रणाम।

मुक्ताभूषण, हिमकुन्दवर्ण, वागीश्वरी, बुद्धिप्रदा कवित्वदा को प्रणाम।

जिसकी कृपा से जड़ भी कालिदास समान कवि बन जाता है — उस सरस्वती को प्रणाम।
benefits: Five jewel-verses of Goddess Saraswati for wisdom
Invokes poetic ability equal to kalidasa
Invokes the blessing of all forms of vidya and eloquence
Ideal for students, writers, and artists
Perfect for Vasant Panchami and daily study
description: Among the most cherished prayers to Goddess Saraswati, Saraswati Pancharatnam (सरस्वती पञ्चरत्नम्) stands as five gems of devotional verses. Drawn from Adi Shankaracharya, this text has been cherished by devotees for centuries. Devotees recite this prayer to grant poetic ability equal to Kalidasa and bestow all forms of vidya and eloquence. This sacred text is ideally recited on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Thursday (Guruvar) and during Vasant Panchami for enhanced blessings. With 5 verses and a reading time of about 3 minutes, this prayer is ideal for both daily worship and special occasions. On this page, you can read the complete Saraswati Pancharatnam in Devanagari Sanskrit with English transliteration, Hindi meaning (arth), and free PDF download for offline recitation.
seoDescription: Read Saraswati Pancharatnam in Sanskrit with Hindi meaning. Five jewel-verses of Saraswati for wisdom and poetic ability.

seoFaqs.question: What is the Saraswati Pancharatnam and who composed it?
seoFaqs.answer: The Saraswati Pancharatnam (सरस्वती पञ्चरत्नम्) is a five-jewel-verse Sanskrit hymn in praise of Goddess Saraswati, traditionally attributed to Adi Shankaracharya (788-820 CE) — the great Advaita Vedanta master and prolific composer of Sanskrit stotras. The five verses each praise a different aspect of Vagdevi: verse 1 invokes her as Vidya-da (giver of knowledge), seated on a white lotus, white-clad, vina-and-pustaka-bearing; verse 2 as Kavitva-da (giver of poetic ability), pearl-ornamented, snowy-jasmine-hued, Brahma-rupa Vagishvari; verse 3 as Sharadambe (Mother Sharada), nectar-speech-showering, fortune-giver, lotus-garlanded; verse 4 as Jnana-da (giver of knowledge), hamsa-mounted, devotee-distress-removing, Nada-Brahma-svarupini, mother of all shastras; verse 5 the famous Kalidasa-promise verse — by her grace even a dull person becomes a poet equal to Kalidasa. (Sources: Adi Shankaracharya stotra collection; Sankara-Granthavali.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the famous Kalidasa promise in the Saraswati Pancharatnam?
seoFaqs.answer: The most celebrated verse of the Saraswati Pancharatnam is verse 5: Yasyah prasadam-avalambya jadopi vakta Kalidasa-vara-tulya-kavishcha bhuyat — meaning by depending upon Her grace, even a dull and dumb person (jada) becomes an eloquent speaker (vakta) and a poet equal to the great Kalidasa (Kalidasa-vara-tulya-kavi). This verse is the single most quoted promise in the entire Saraswati stotra corpus and is recited by aspiring poets, writers, students, scholars, orators, musicians, and creative artists worldwide. The story behind it is the famous legend of how Kalidasa himself — said to have been a complete jada (dullard) in childhood — received Saraswati's direct grace at her Kali temple and instantly became the greatest poet of Sanskrit literature, author of Abhijnana-Shakuntalam, Meghaduta, Raghuvansha and Kumarasambhava. (Sources: Saraswati Pancharatnam verse 5; Kalidasa-katha tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: When should the Saraswati Pancharatnam be recited?
seoFaqs.answer: The Saraswati Pancharatnam is most powerful when recited on Wednesday (Budhvar) and Thursday (Guruvar). The most auspicious days of the year are: Vasant Panchami / Saraswati Puja (Magha Shukla Panchami — the chief Saraswati festival when poets, students and musicians worship their instruments and books), Sharad Navaratri (especially the Saraswati Avahana / Mula Nakshatra day and the Saraswati Puja on day 7), Vijayadashami / Vidyarambha Day (when children begin formal learning), Akshaya Tritiya, exam mornings, and the first day of any new course of study, book, manuscript, composition or creative work. The phala-shruti specifically promises that one who recites it at dawn (pratar-utthitah) becomes a kavi-raja (king among poets) endowed with sarva-vidya. Daily morning chanting after bath, before opening books, is the prescribed practice. (Sources: Drikpanchang; Adi Shankaracharya stotra parayanam tradition.)

seoFaqs.question: What is the difference between Saraswati Pancharatnam and Saraswati Panchakam?
seoFaqs.answer: Both are five-verse Saraswati hymns but differ in authorship, depth and ritual emphasis. The Saraswati Pancharatnam is attributed to Adi Shankaracharya — densely packed with Advaita-Vedanta theological epithets (Brahma-rupa, Nada-Brahma-svarupini, sarva-shastra-mata, Vagishvari) and culminating in the famous Kalidasa-equal-poet promise of verse 5; it is more philosophical and is a favourite among poets, scholars and Vedantins. The Saraswati Panchakam is a traditional five-verse composition focused more on iconographic devotion (white lotus, white garments, vina, hamsa, four arms, pustaka, akshamala) with a phalashruti promising that Vagdevi resides on the tongue of the daily reciter. Both are recited together at Vasant Panchami, Sharad Navaratri Saraswati Puja, and Vidyarambha. (Source: classical Saraswati stotra corpus.)

seoFaqs.question: What benefits does the Saraswati Pancharatnam grant?
seoFaqs.answer: The phala-shruti declares: Saraswati-pancharatnam yah pathet pratar-utthitah, sarva-vidyam samasadya kaviraajo bhaven-narah — the man who recites this Saraswati Pancharatnam at dawn obtains all forms of vidya (sarva-vidya) and becomes a king among poets (kaviraja). Tradition further attributes the following: (1) Vidya-prapti — mastery in all branches of knowledge (vyakarana, jyotisha, nyaya, vedanta, shilpa, sangeeta, nritya, kavya), (2) kavitva-shakti — the awakening of original poetic and creative ability, (3) buddhi-vridhi — sharpening of intellect and memory, (4) vakpatutva — eloquence in speech and writing, (5) examination success and academic excellence for students, (6) creative inspiration for writers, poets, musicians, dancers, painters, sculptors and all artists, (7) the supreme blessing of becoming Kalidasa-vara-tulya — equal to the great Kalidasa. (Source: Saraswati Pancharatnam phalashruti.)

seoFaqs.question: Why does Adi Shankaracharya address Saraswati as Brahma-rupa and Nada-Brahma-svarupini?
seoFaqs.answer: In Adi Shankara's Advaita Vedanta vision, every deity ultimately points to the one Para-Brahman — the non-dual supreme reality. Saraswati as Brahma-rupa (literally Brahman-form) signifies that she is not a limited deity but the very form of Brahman manifest as vidya, vak and consciousness — knowledge is the very nature of the Self. Nada-Brahma-svarupini (whose form is Nada-Brahman, the Sound-Brahman) identifies her with the cosmic primordial vibration from which all language, music, mantra and creation arise — the Pranava OM is itself Nada-Brahman, and Saraswati is its goddess-form. This Advaita identification justifies why Shankara, the great non-dualist, could compose stotras to personal deities — because every Devi-stuti is ultimately Brahma-stuti when read through Advaita eyes. (Sources: Sankara-Bhashya on Brahma Sutras; Saundarya Lahari; Saraswati Pancharatnam.)
